description: 5 reference sets of Take an Interview questions, each with 4 questions in progressive difficulty. All on a single topic with scenario intro framing. 45 seconds response time per question, no prep time.

patterns.progression: Q1 personal/factual -> Q2 descriptive/comparative -> Q3 analytical/opinion -> Q4 evaluative/predictive
patterns.intro_template: You have agreed to participate in a [study/survey/research project] about [topic].

sets.intro: You have agreed to participate in a survey about digital subscription services.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our survey. What types of digital subscription services do you currently use, and how did you first start using them?
How do you decide whether to continue paying for a subscription service or cancel it? What factors matter most to you?
Some people argue that subscription services save money in the long run, while others feel they lead to overspending. What is your perspective on this?
Looking ahead, how do you think the growth of digital subscriptions will change the way people consume entertainment and information in the next decade?

sets.intro: You have agreed to participate in a research project about adult learning and hobbies.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in this study. Have you recently taken up any new hobbies or learned a new skill as an adult? Please describe your experience.
What challenges do adults typically face when trying to learn something new compared to when they were younger? Can you give some examples?
Some experts believe that adults who regularly pursue new hobbies are more creative and adaptable at work. Do you agree or disagree with this claim?
If a company wanted to encourage employees to develop new skills outside of work, what kinds of programs or incentives would you recommend they offer?

sets.intro: You have agreed to participate in a survey about public speaking experiences.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our survey. Can you describe a recent situation where you had to speak in front of a group? How did it go?
What do you think are the biggest differences between people who feel comfortable speaking publicly and those who find it very stressful?
Some educators argue that public speaking skills should be a required part of every student's education. What is your opinion on this proposal?
With the increasing use of virtual meetings and online presentations, how do you think public speaking skills and expectations will change in the future?

sets.intro: You have agreed to participate in a study about meal planning and grocery shopping.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in this study. How do you usually decide what to eat each week? Do you plan your meals in advance or decide day by day?
What are some advantages and disadvantages of planning meals for the entire week compared to shopping for groceries on a daily basis?
Some nutritionists claim that people who plan their meals in advance tend to eat healthier and waste less food. To what extent do you agree with this claim?
If the government wanted to help families eat healthier while spending less on groceries, what policies or programs would you suggest they implement?

sets.intro: You have agreed to participate in a research project about workplace schedules and flexibility.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our research. What does your current work or study schedule look like, and how much flexibility do you have in setting your own hours?
How does having a flexible schedule compare to having a fixed schedule in terms of productivity and personal well-being? What are the trade-offs?
Some companies have adopted a four-day workweek to improve employee satisfaction without reducing output. Do you think this approach is effective? Why or why not?
As remote work becomes more common, how do you think workplace scheduling norms will evolve over the next five to ten years, and what impact will this have on workers?
